name: Guadiamar Green Corridor Loop
name_original: Río  Guadiamar – circular desde la Laguna de las Dobla por Corta de los Frailes y Aznalcóllar
description: Explore the Guadiamar Green Corridor on this 28.8-mile mountain bike loop, featuring Laguna de las Doblas and views of Corta de los Frailes.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sanlúcar La Mayor offers diverse terrain within the Aljarafe region of Seville. The area is characterized by the Guadiamar Green Corridor, which follows the Guadiamar River, featuring wetlands and Mediterranean forest. Riders can navigate through extensive olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields, providing varied landscapes. The topography includes a natural platform created by two gully arms, adding unique elements to the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Sanlúcar La Mayor The most popular mountain bike trail is Río Guadiamar – circular desde la Laguna de las Dobla por Corta de los Frailes y Aznalcóllar, a 28.8 miles (46.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Guadiamar Green Corridor, passing through varied natural environments. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Río Guadiamar - vuelta desde Parking Las Doblas, an easy 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This route offers a more accessible ride along the Guadiamar River, suitable for those seeking a less strenuous experience. Local mountain bikers also love the Laguna de las Doblas – Guadiamar Ford loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through the Laguna de las Doblas area, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Sanlúcar La Mayor is defined by the Guadiamar Green Corridor, extensive olive groves, and varied riverine landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 1600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sanlúcar La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Sanlúcar La Mayor offers a variety of mountain bike trails, ranging from easy paths suitable for beginners to more challenging moderate routes. The terrain often includes extensive olive groves, vineyards, cereal fields, and riverine landscapes, particularly within the Guadiamar Green Corridor. You can expect a mix of paved and unpaved segments, with some routes featuring significant ascents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Yes, there are several easy mountain bike trails in the area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993038060/rio-guadiamar-vuelta-desde-parking-las-doblas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Guadiamar - vuelta desde Parking Las Doblas&lt;/a&gt; is an accessible 9.6-mile (15.5 km) path along the Guadiamar River, offering a less strenuous experience with minimal elevation gain.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails available around Sanlúcar La Mayor, catering to various skill levels. This includes 6 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for every rider.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor frequently traverse the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the Guadiamar River, wetlands, and Mediterranean forest. You'll also ride through picturesque olive groves, vineyards, and cereal fields, characteristic of the Aljarafe region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7389130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Doblas Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural spot with boardwalks and lagoons.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas – Guadiamar Ford loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.9-mile (43.3 km) trail that takes you through the Laguna de las Doblas area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sanlúcar La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 120 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths within the Guadiamar Green Corridor, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region has a rich historical heritage. You might encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1606627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge Over the Pudio River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7086624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Loreto&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself features medieval churches and historic haciendas, offering cultural points of interest to complement your ride.

Q: Is it possible to find food and drink options along the trails?
A: While many routes are through natural areas, you can find facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2858510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cafeteria Camping Dehesa Nueva&lt;/a&gt;. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes, and plan your stops in advance.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: The climate in Sanlúcar La Mayor is Mediterranean, making spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often lush. Summers can be very hot, so if riding during this season, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, some routes offer a longer challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33767894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Footbridge – Guadiamar Ford loop from Sanlúcar la Mayor&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.8-mile (51.1 km) trail that provides an extended ride through the diverse landscapes of the region.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with other outdoor activities in Sanlúcar La Mayor?
A: Absolutely. The natural surroundings of Sanlúcar La Mayor are also excellent for hiking and horse riding. The Guadiamar Green Corridor is a prime spot for birdwatching, and hot air balloon rides are available for a unique aerial perspective of the Aljarafe region.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993038060/rio-guadiamar-vuelta-desde-parking-las-doblas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Guadiamar - vuelta desde Parking Las Doblas&lt;/a&gt;, start from designated parking areas like the one at Las Doblas. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.
tips: Visit in spring. Easily accessible by bicycle with parking for a vehicle very close.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Río Guadiamar trail is generally considered easy and suitable for families and beginners. It features a largely flat profile with pressed earth paths, making it a comfortable ride. While some sections may have natural loose surfaces or singletrack, the overall experience is relaxed.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of pressed earth paths, often described as 'practically flat.' You will also encounter some short cemented sections at river crossings, as well as natural loose surfaces, singletrack, and paved segments. It's a varied but generally smooth ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Río Guadiamar mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from the Laguna de las Doblas, which is a good starting point. The area around the Guadiamar Green Corridor often has recreational areas with designated parking spots, making it convenient to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is the Río Guadiamar trail a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific route, 'Río Guadiamar – circular desde la Laguna de las Dobla por Corta de los Frailes y Aznalcóllar,' is a circular trail, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery including the Guadiamar river, gallery forests, open fields, and dehesas with wild olive and lentisk trees. Key highlights include the beautifully restored Laguna de las Doblas with its wooden walkway and aquatic birds, and the View of Corta de los Frailes. You might also spot Horses on the Trail and pass over the Guadiamar Footbridge and the Narrow Bridge of the Guadiamar.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Río Guadiamar trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during the cooler months, such as autumn, winter, and spring, to avoid the intense summer heat of Andalusia. These seasons offer more pleasant temperatures for cycling and enjoying the natural surroundings.

faqs.question: Is the Río Guadiamar trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Guadiamar Green Corridor is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike in the Paisaje Protegido Corredor Verde del Guadiamar?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access and mountain bike along the trails within the Paisaje Protegido Corredor Verde del Guadiamar. It is a protected natural area open for public enjoyment, but always respect local regulations regarding conservation and use.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes and intersects with several official trails within the Guadiamar Green Corridor. You will ride sections of the Corredor Verde, Cordel de los Carboneros, Camino del Tizón, and even a small part of the Vía Verde de Itálica, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Guadiamar Green Corridor is rich in wildlife. You can expect to see abundant aquatic birdlife, especially around the Laguna de las Doblas and at various bird observation points along the river. Wild horses are also known to graze freely in the area, adding to the natural charm.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available near the trail, such as cafes or bike rentals?
faqs.answer: The route passes near Aznalcóllar, where you can find local amenities like cafes and possibly accommodation. While specific bike rental shops directly at the trailhead might be limited, Aznalcóllar would be the closest town to inquire about such services.

waypoints.name: View of Corta de los Frailes
